description: theta sensitivity of the 34 bridge theorems and 2/8-strict/6/8-loose historical recovery (q-0012). Sweep theta in {1,2,3,5}; for each, count surviving bridge theorems, Jaccard/nesting vs the theta=2 set, and strict historical recovery (8 known bridges). Clusters read from cache; pure CPU.
motivation: Reviewers JAGf, HJ3i: theta=2 is the single most load-bearing threshold and is never stress-tested. Test whether the bridge set and historical hits persist off theta=2.
formal_results: For each theta, a bridge theorem survives iff min in-degree over its cross-cluster (>=2 distinct pure-cluster) premises <= theta. Strict historical recovery = a known-bridge name pattern matches some surviving theorem; loose = matches the canonical 280-set bridge_theorems.json (theta-independent). Jaccard(S_theta, S_2) and subset/superset relations recorded.
results_description: Headline reproduces exactly: theta=2 -> 34 bridges, 2/8 strict, 6/8 loose. Count is HIGHLY theta-sensitive and monotone/nested: 15 (theta=1) / 34 (theta=2) / 60 (theta=3) / 72 (theta=5). Jaccard vs theta=2 set: 0.44 / 1.0 / 0.57 / 0.47 -- the exact 34-bridge set is not stable to +-1 in theta (subset at theta=1, superset at theta>=3). Historical STRICT recovery is the robust part: 2/8 for theta in {2,3,5}, dropping to 1/8 only at theta=1. The same two bridges are recovered at theta>=2: Gromov-Hausdorff and Spectral<->Representation. Only Spectral<->Representation is strict-stable across ALL theta incl 1; Gromov-Hausdorff is the single flip (lost at theta=1). Loose 6/8 is theta-independent by construction.
conclusions: The 34-count headline is a resolution-relative artifact of theta=2 (the elbow): it roughly doubles by theta=3, so the specific number should not be reported as load-bearing. But the SCIENTIFIC claim -- strict premise-based detection recovers ~2/8 celebrated bridges (specifically Gromov-Hausdorff and Spectral<->Representation) -- is robust across theta>=2. theta=1 is too aggressive (loses Gromov-Hausdorff). theta=2 sits at the low end of the stable plateau, so it is defensible as the strict operating point even though the bridge count it yields is fragile.
